page: The Microsoft Azure D8ls_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8573B processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This server does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2178.0 placing it in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 31.36 GB/sec, and Geekbench text processing scores reach 2723.0. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8357.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 7969.36. For small language model inference using a 135M model, text generation reaches a strong 283.15 tokens/sec, though larger workloads like a 7B model drop to a weak 7.37 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D8ls_v6 offers a compute-heavy profile with a lower memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a resource-dense option for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. The lack of local storage means users must rely on external Premium Storage, which introduces an architectural tradeoff.

This server is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, web serving, single-threaded data processing, and lightweight machine learning inference. It is also appropriate for general-purpose applications with low memory requirements.
description: The Microsoft Azure D8ls_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon 8573B processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, operating at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per vCPU) and lacks local storage, though it supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2178.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression at 31.36 GB/sec. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally align with mid-tier averages. This server is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy, low-memory workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and lightweight text or language model processing, though it is constrained by its 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio and reliance on external storage.
og_description: The Microsoft Azure D8ls_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon 8573B processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: The Microsoft Azure D8ls_v6 is an x86_64 server featuring 8 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring high-frequency Intel processors and a low memory-to-core ratio for dedicated CPU workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573B processor running at 4.2 GHz
8 dedicated vCPUs with 16.0 GB of RAM (1:2 ratio)
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench testing
No local storage; Premium Storage capable
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight inference
